name: Jump Squat
description: Explosive squat variation that builds lower body power and speed.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart
Lower into a squat until thighs are parallel to the floor
Explode upward and jump as high as possible
Land softly with bent knees
Immediately lower into the next rep

name: Bulgarian Pin Squat
description: Single-leg squat with rear foot elevated, targeting quads and glutes.
instructions: Place rear foot on a bench behind you, front foot forward
Stand upright with core engaged
Lower until front thigh is parallel to the floor
Push through front heel to return to start
Complete all reps on one side, then switch

name: Split Squat Calf Raises
description: Combines a split squat position with calf raises for lower leg strength.
instructions: Stand in a split squat stance, front foot forward
Lower into a split squat position and hold
Rise up onto the front toes in a calf raise
Lower the heel back down with control
Repeat for target reps, then switch sides

name: Boden Walks
description: Walking lunges performed across the floor for leg and glute endurance.
instructions: Stand upright with feet together
Step forward with one foot and lower back knee toward the floor
Keep front knee above ankle and torso upright
Push off back foot and step into the next lunge
Continue alternating legs across the floor

name: Sprints
description: Short, all-out running efforts to build speed and cardiovascular power.
instructions: Warm up with 5 minutes of light jogging
Drive knees high and pump arms aggressively
Sprint at 80–100% effort for 20–40 meters
Walk back to start for recovery
Repeat 6–10 times

name: Battle Ropes
description: Conditioning exercise using heavy ropes to build power and endurance.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, slight bend in knees
Hold one rope end in each hand
Alternately slam each rope up and down in a wave motion
Keep core tight and maintain a steady rhythm
Perform for 20–40 seconds per set

name: Med Ball Slams
description: Explosive full-body movement that develops power and conditions the whole body.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, hold med ball overhead
Rise onto toes and brace core
Slam the ball into the ground as hard as possible
Catch on the bounce or pick it up
Repeat for 8–12 reps

name: Med Ball Pushes
description: Power throw that trains explosive upper body and core strength.
instructions: Stand facing a wall, hold med ball at chest height
Step forward and explosively push the ball into the wall
Fully extend both arms at the point of release
Catch the rebound and immediately reset
Perform 8–12 explosive reps

name: Sled Pushes
description: Full-body conditioning movement that builds leg drive and work capacity.
instructions: Load the sled with your desired weight
Place hands on the handles and lean forward at about 45°
Drive through your legs in short, powerful steps
Keep core tight and back flat throughout
Push 20–40 meters, rest, then repeat

name: Sled Pulls
description: Pulling movement that builds posterior chain strength and conditioning.
instructions: Attach a harness or rope to the sled
Face away from the sled and lean forward slightly
Drive forward with powerful leg steps while the sled follows
Keep upright posture and engage core
Pull 20–40 meters, rest, then repeat

name: Wall Sit Holds
description: Static lower body hold that builds quad endurance and knee strength.
instructions: Stand with back flat against a wall
Slide down until thighs are parallel to the floor
Keep knees directly above ankles and feet flat
Hold the position without sliding
Aim for 30–60 seconds

name: Wall Sit Holds on Toes
description: Wall sit variation with heels raised to increase calf and ankle engagement.
instructions: Set up in a standard wall sit position
Rise onto your toes while maintaining the squat angle
Keep knees above ankles and back flat against the wall
Hold with weight on the balls of your feet
Aim for 20–45 seconds

name: Wall Sit Holds on Heels
description: Wall sit variation with toes raised to target the tibialis and anterior shin.
instructions: Set up in a standard wall sit position
Lift your toes off the floor, balancing on your heels
Keep knees above ankles and back flat against the wall
Maintain heel contact throughout the hold
Aim for 20–45 seconds

name: Squat Holds
description: Static hold at the bottom of a squat to build quad and hip strength.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly out
Lower into a deep squat until thighs are parallel or below
Keep chest up, heels flat, and knees over toes
Hold the bottom position without bouncing
Aim for 20–60 seconds

name: ATG Split Squat Holds
description: Deep split squat hold that stretches and strengthens hip flexors and quads.
instructions: Step one foot forward and lower back knee to the floor
Sink as deep as possible into the front knee bend
Keep front shin vertical and torso upright
Hold the deep position with control
Aim for 15–30 seconds per side

name: Lunge Holds
description: Static lunge position that challenges quad, glute, and hip flexor strength.
instructions: Step one foot forward into a lunge stance
Lower back knee toward the floor without touching it
Keep front knee above ankle and torso upright
Hold with both legs engaged
Aim for 20–40 seconds per side

name: Single Leg Holds
description: Single-leg balance hold to strengthen stabilizers and improve balance.
instructions: Stand on one leg with a slight bend in the knee
Lift the opposite foot a few inches off the ground
Keep hips level and core engaged
Focus on a fixed point ahead to help balance
Hold for 30–60 seconds per side

name: Single Leg Holds (Knee Up)
description: Single-leg hold with the free knee raised to hip height for hip flexor engagement.
instructions: Stand on one leg with a slight bend in the standing knee
Raise the opposite knee to hip height at 90°
Keep hips square and core tight
Hold without letting the raised hip drop
Aim for 20–40 seconds per side

name: Knee Extension Holds (Band)
description: Isometric quad strengthening using a resistance band at a fixed knee angle.
instructions: Sit on a bench with a resistance band looped around your ankle
Extend your leg against the band resistance
Stop at your chosen angle (e.g., 60° or fully extended)
Hold with the quad fully contracted
Aim for 20–40 seconds per side

name: Knee External Rotation Holds
description: Isometric hold that strengthens the hip external rotators.
instructions: Sit or stand with knees bent at 90°
Press the knee outward against a fixed resistance (hand or band)
Keep hips level and spine neutral
Hold the outward pressure without the knee moving
Aim for 20–30 seconds per side

name: Knee Internal Rotation Holds
description: Isometric hold that strengthens the hip internal rotators.
instructions: Sit or stand with knees bent at 90°
Press the knee inward against a fixed resistance (hand or band)
Keep hips level and spine neutral
Hold the inward pressure without the knee moving
Aim for 20–30 seconds per side

name: Spanish Squat (Band)
description: Band-assisted squat hold that targets the quads with reduced shear force.
instructions: Loop a resistance band around a fixed post at shin height
Step back so the band rests against your shins
Sit back into a squat while the band supports the shins
Lower until thighs are parallel, keeping torso upright
Hold for 20–40 seconds

name: Glute Bridge Holds
description: Isometric glute hold at the top of a bridge position.
instructions: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor
Drive hips upward by squeezing glutes until body forms a straight line
Keep core tight and avoid hyperextending the lower back
Hold the top position with glutes fully contracted
Aim for 20–40 seconds

name: RDL Holds
description: Isometric hinge hold at the bottom of an RDL to strengthen hamstrings.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart, holding weights
Hinge at hips, pushing them back while lowering the weights
Stop when you feel a strong hamstring stretch (around shin level)
Keep back flat and a slight bend in the knees
Hold for 15–30 seconds

name: Single Leg RDL Holds
description: Single-leg hinge hold that challenges hamstrings and hip stabilizers.
instructions: Stand on one leg with a slight bend in the knee
Hinge forward at the hip, extending the free leg behind you
Lower torso until roughly parallel to the floor, keep back flat
Hold with core tight and hips square
Aim for 15–30 seconds per side

name: Seated Good Morning Holds
description: Seated isometric hold that strengthens the lower back and hamstrings.
instructions: Sit on a bench with feet flat on floor
Hinge forward at the hips, lowering chest toward thighs
Keep back flat and core braced throughout
Hold the forward lean position
Aim for 20–30 seconds

name: Kettlebell Hip Holds
description: Isometric hip hold with a kettlebell to build hip and glute strength.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart, kettlebell hanging in front
Hinge slightly at the hips and squeeze glutes to hold the position
Keep shoulders back and core engaged
Maintain the partial hinge angle
Hold for 20–40 seconds

name: Hip Abduction Holds
description: Isometric hold that strengthens the hip abductors and outer glutes.
instructions: Lie on your side or stand near a wall with a band around your ankles
Lift or press the top leg outward against the resistance
Keep hips stacked and core engaged
Hold the abducted position without rotating the hip
Aim for 20–30 seconds per side

name: Adductor Holds
description: Isometric inner thigh hold to strengthen the adductors.
instructions: Lie on your back with knees bent and a ball between your knees
Squeeze the ball between your knees with maximal force
Keep lower back flat on the floor
Hold the squeeze without holding your breath
Aim for 20–40 seconds

name: Copenhagen Plank
description: Side plank variation with the top leg on a bench, targeting the adductors and core.
instructions: Place your top foot on a bench and let the bottom leg hang
Press into a side plank position with your elbow on the floor
Lift your hips until your body forms a straight line
Keep core tight and hips elevated
Hold for 15–30 seconds per side

name: Plank
description: Fundamental core exercise that builds full-body stability and endurance.
instructions: Start on forearms and toes, elbows directly below shoulders
Keep body in a straight line from head to heels
Brace core, squeeze glutes, and breathe steadily
Avoid letting hips sag or rise
Hold for 30–60 seconds

name: Side Plank
description: Lateral core exercise that strengthens the obliques and hip stabilizers.
instructions: Lie on your side with elbow below shoulder
Stack feet or stagger them for balance
Lift hips off the floor until body forms a straight line
Keep core tight and hips elevated throughout
Hold for 20–40 seconds per side

name: Leg Holds
description: Isometric lower ab exercise performed lying on your back.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with hands under your lower back
Raise both legs to about 15 cm above the floor
Keep lower back pressed into the floor
Hold the position without letting legs drop
Aim for 20–40 seconds

name: Scissors
description: Alternating leg movement that targets the lower abs and hip flexors.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with legs extended
Raise both legs about 15 cm off the floor
Alternately cross one leg over the other in a scissors motion
Keep lower back pressed into the floor throughout
Perform for 20–40 seconds

name: Biceps Curl Holds
description: Isometric biceps hold at a fixed elbow angle to build static arm strength.
instructions: Hold a dumbbell with underhand grip, curl to 90°
Keep elbows close to your sides and upper arms still
Hold the position while maintaining tension in the biceps
Breathe steadily, do not hold your breath
Aim for 20–30 seconds per arm

name: Gewichtsscheibe Halten
description: Weight plate hold that builds grip, shoulder, and forearm endurance.
instructions: Hold a weight plate at chest height with both hands on the edges
Keep arms extended or slightly bent
Engage shoulders and core to maintain the position
Do not let the plate sag below chest level
Hold for 20–40 seconds

name: Swings
description: Foundational kettlebell movement that develops explosive hip power and posterior chain strength.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart, kettlebell between feet
Hinge at hips and grip the kettlebell with both hands
Hike the kettlebell back between your legs
Drive hips forward explosively to swing the bell to chest height
Let it swing back and repeat for 15–20 reps

name: Alternating Swings
description: Single-arm swing variation that adds rotational challenge and grip training.
instructions: Perform a standard two-handed swing to chest height
At the top, release with one hand and catch with the other
Let the bell swing back between your legs
Drive hips forward and repeat the hand switch each rep
Perform 10–15 reps per side

name: Side Swings
description: Lateral kettlebell swing that targets the hips and adds rotational power.
instructions: Stand with feet wider than hip-width apart
Swing the kettlebell to one side at hip height
Let it pendulum across to the other side
Rotate hips slightly in the direction of each swing
Perform 10–12 reps per side

name: Single Leg Deadlift
description: Unilateral hip hinge with a kettlebell that strengthens hamstrings and balance.
instructions: Stand on one leg, holding the kettlebell in the opposite hand
Hinge forward at the hips, extending the free leg behind you
Lower the kettlebell toward the floor, keeping back flat
Drive through the standing heel to return upright
Perform 8–10 reps per side

name: Bob and Wave
description: Dynamic full-body kettlebell movement combining a hinge with a shoulder motion.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, kettlebell in both hands
Hinge forward into a partial hip hinge (the bob)
Add a wave motion with the arms while staying in the hinge position
Keep core tight and back flat throughout
Perform for 10–15 reps

name: Lunge + Alternating Presses
description: Combines a lunge with an overhead press to train full-body coordination and strength.
instructions: Hold a kettlebell in one hand at shoulder height
Step into a lunge with the opposite leg forward
As you lower into the lunge, press the kettlebell overhead
Return to standing as you lower the bell
Alternate sides for 8–10 reps per side

name: Racked Squat
description: Squat with kettlebell in rack position that challenges core stability and leg strength.
instructions: Hold the kettlebell in rack position (resting on forearm, elbow tucked)
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly out
Lower into a squat keeping the bell stable and torso upright
Drive through heels to return to standing
Perform 8–12 reps per side

name: Goblet Squat
description: Front-loaded squat holding the kettlebell at chest height for depth and posture.
instructions: Hold kettlebell by the horns at chest height
Stand with feet slightly wider than hip-width, toes out
Lower into a deep squat keeping elbows inside your knees
Keep chest up and heels flat on the floor
Drive through heels to stand — 10–15 reps

name: Dual Front Squat
description: Double kettlebell front squat for increased load and upper back engagement.
instructions: Clean two kettlebells into rack position at shoulder height
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart
Lower into a deep squat keeping bells stable in rack
Drive through heels to return to standing
Perform 6–10 reps

name: Lunge
description: Kettlebell lunge that adds load for greater leg and glute development.
instructions: Hold a kettlebell in each hand hanging at sides or one in rack position
Step one foot forward and lower back knee toward the floor
Keep front knee above ankle and torso upright
Push through front heel to return to start
Perform 8–12 reps per side

name: Alternating Lunges
description: Alternating forward lunges with kettlebell for leg endurance and balance.
instructions: Hold a kettlebell in each hand hanging at sides
Step forward with one leg into a lunge
Lower until back knee nearly touches the floor
Push back to standing and immediately lunge with the other leg
Continue alternating for 10–12 reps per side

name: Side Lunges
description: Lateral lunge with kettlebell that targets inner thighs and glutes.
instructions: Hold kettlebell at chest height or hanging in front
Step wide to one side, bending the stepping knee
Lower hips toward the floor while keeping the other leg straight
Push off the bent leg to return to center
Perform 8–10 reps per side

name: Reverse Lunge Swings
description: Combines a reverse lunge with a kettlebell swing for power and coordination.
instructions: Start standing with kettlebell in both hands
Step back into a reverse lunge as you hike the bell between your legs
Drive the front foot into the floor and extend the hip to come out of the lunge
Use the hip drive to swing the bell to chest height
Alternate legs for 8–10 reps per side

name: Lunges Around the World
description: Full lunge circuit covering forward, lateral, and reverse directions.
instructions: Hold kettlebells at sides
Lunge forward with one leg and return to center
Lunge to the side with the same leg and return to center
Lunge backward with the same leg and return to center
Complete all directions on one side, then switch — 3 rounds per side

name: Wood Chopper Lunges
description: Lunge combined with a rotational wood chop for full-body movement.
instructions: Hold a kettlebell with both hands above one shoulder
Step into a lunge as you bring the bell diagonally to the opposite hip
Keep core engaged and rotate through the torso
Return to standing as you raise the bell back overhead
Perform 8–10 reps per side

name: Clean Arm Press
description: Two-phase kettlebell movement combining a clean and a press for power and strength.
instructions: Hinge at hips with kettlebell between legs
Drive hips forward and pull the bell into rack position (the clean)
From rack, press the bell overhead until arm is fully extended
Lower back to rack, then to starting position
Perform 6–8 reps per side

name: Horizontal Presses
description: Horizontal pushing exercise with kettlebells to develop chest and triceps.
instructions: Lie on a bench or floor holding a kettlebell in each hand at chest level
Start with elbows at about 45° from the body
Press both bells straight up until arms are extended
Lower with control back to chest level
Perform 8–12 reps

name: Shoulder Presses
description: Overhead press with kettlebell for shoulder and upper body strength.
instructions: Hold kettlebell in rack position at shoulder height
Brace core and keep elbow tucked below the bell
Press the bell straight overhead until arm is fully extended
Lower slowly back to rack position
Perform 8–10 reps per side

name: Pullovers
description: Lying pullover with kettlebell that stretches the chest and strengthens the lats.
instructions: Lie on a bench with head near the edge, holding kettlebell above chest
Keeping arms slightly bent, lower the bell behind your head
Feel the stretch across chest and lats
Pull the bell back over chest to starting position
Perform 10–12 reps

name: Rows
description: Single-arm kettlebell row that builds upper back and bicep strength.
instructions: Place one hand and knee on a bench for support
Hold kettlebell in the other hand, arm extended toward floor
Pull the bell toward your hip, driving elbow back
Keep back flat and avoid rotating the torso
Lower with control — 8–12 reps per side

name: Upright Rows
description: Vertical pull targeting the upper traps and side deltoids.
instructions: Stand holding dumbbells or barbell in front of thighs
Pull weight straight up along your body toward the chin
Lead with your elbows, keeping them above the wrists
Do not pull higher than the chin
Lower with control — 10–12 reps

name: Side Front Raises
description: Combination of lateral and front raises to develop shoulder endurance.
instructions: Stand holding a kettlebell in each hand at sides
Raise one arm to the side and the other to the front simultaneously
Lift to shoulder height with a slight elbow bend
Lower with control and alternate arms
Perform 10–12 reps per side

name: Rotation
description: Kettlebell rotation exercise that develops core rotational strength.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding kettlebell at chest
Rotate the torso to one side, bringing the bell along
Keep hips square and feet flat on the floor
Return to center and rotate to the other side
Perform 10–12 reps per side

name: Windmills
description: Complex movement that develops shoulder stability, hip mobility, and core strength.
instructions: Press kettlebell overhead with one arm, feet shoulder-width apart
Turn feet 45° away from the arm holding the bell
Hinge sideways at the hip, lowering the free hand toward the floor
Keep eyes on the kettlebell throughout
Return to standing — 5–8 reps per side

name: Uppercuts
description: Boxing-inspired kettlebell movement that trains rotational power and shoulder strength.
instructions: Stand in a slight squat stance, holding a kettlebell in one hand
Drive the bell upward in an uppercut arc, rotating through the hips
Let hips and core generate the power, not just the arm
Control the bell back to starting position
Perform 10–12 reps per side

name: Helos
description: Circular overhead kettlebell movement that challenges shoulder stability and core control.
instructions: Press the kettlebell overhead with one arm
Move the bell in a controlled circle overhead (halo motion)
Keep the shoulder packed and core braced
Complete 5 circles in one direction, then reverse
Perform per side with fully controlled movement

name: Kniend Pushout Rotate
description: Kneeling kettlebell push-and-rotate exercise for core anti-rotation strength.
instructions: Kneel on one knee, holding kettlebell at chest with both hands
Push the bell straight out in front with arms extended
Rotate the bell to the left then to the right while arms stay extended
Return to chest and repeat
Perform 8–10 reps per side

name: Squats
description: Fundamental lower body exercise targeting quads, glutes, and hamstrings.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly out
Brace core and push hips back as you lower down
Lower until thighs are parallel to the floor or deeper
Keep chest up and heels flat on the floor
Drive through heels to return to standing — 8–12 reps

name: Lunges
description: Unilateral leg exercise for quad, glute, and balance development.
instructions: Stand with feet together, hands on hips or holding weights
Step one foot forward and lower back knee toward the floor
Keep front knee above ankle and torso upright
Push off front foot to return to starting position
Alternate legs for 10–12 reps per side

name: ATG Split Squats
description: Deep range split squat reaching full hip and knee flexion for maximum leg development.
instructions: Step one foot forward into a lunge stance
Lower the back knee all the way to the floor
Lean the front shin forward to maximize depth
Drive through front foot to return to start
Perform 8–10 reps per side

name: Split Squats
description: Stationary lunge that isolates each leg individually for strength and balance.
instructions: Stand in a split stance with one foot forward, one back
Lower straight down by bending both knees
Keep front knee tracking over the toes
Drive through front heel to return to start
Perform 8–12 reps per side

name: RDL's
description: Hip hinge movement targeting the hamstrings and glutes with a slight knee bend.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart, holding weights in front of thighs
Push hips back and lower the weights along your legs
Stop when you feel a strong hamstring stretch
Keep back flat and a slight bend in the knees
Drive hips forward to return — 8–12 reps

name: Single Leg RDL's
description: Single-leg hip hinge that challenges balance while targeting hamstrings and glutes.
instructions: Stand on one leg with a slight bend in the knee
Hinge forward at the hip, extending the free leg behind for balance
Lower weight toward the floor while keeping back flat
Return to standing by driving through the heel
Perform 8–10 reps per side

name: Tibialis Raises
description: Isolation exercise for the tibialis anterior (shin muscle) to prevent shin splints.
instructions: Stand with heels on a step or against a wall
Lift the front of your feet (toes toward shins) as high as possible
Hold briefly at the top, then lower with control
Keep legs straight throughout the movement
Perform 15–20 reps

name: Leg Curls
description: Machine or band exercise that isolates the hamstrings.
instructions: Lie face down on a leg curl machine with ankles under the pad
Curl both legs up toward your glutes
Squeeze hamstrings at the top
Lower with control back to start
Perform 10–15 reps

name: Leg Extension
description: Quad isolation exercise performed on a machine.
instructions: Sit on a leg extension machine with ankles behind the pad
Extend both legs until straight, contracting the quads
Hold briefly at the top
Lower with control back to start
Perform 10–15 reps

name: Calf Raises
description: Isolation exercise for the calves to build lower leg strength and size.
instructions: Stand with the balls of your feet on an elevated surface
Lower heels as far as possible for a full stretch
Drive up onto your toes as high as possible
Hold at the top for a brief squeeze
Perform 15–20 reps

name: Romanian Deadlift
description: Hip hinge movement targeting the hamstrings and lower back.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart, barbell or dumbbells in front
Hinge at hips keeping back flat and bar close to legs
Lower until a strong stretch is felt in the hamstrings
Drive hips forward to return to standing
Perform 8–12 reps

name: Single Leg Step Ups
description: Unilateral step-up onto a box to develop quad and glute strength.
instructions: Stand in front of a sturdy box or step
Place one foot fully on the box
Drive through that heel to lift your body onto the box
Step back down with control
Perform 10–12 reps per side

name: Lat Pull Downs
description: Cable exercise that builds upper back width by training the latissimus dorsi.
instructions: Sit at a lat pulldown machine, grip bar wider than shoulder-width
Lean slightly back and pull the bar down to upper chest level
Squeeze shoulder blades together at the bottom
Extend arms slowly to return to start
Perform 10–12 reps

name: Rows Tief
description: Low horizontal row targeting the middle and lower back.
instructions: Sit at a cable row station, grip the handle with both hands
Keep back straight and pull handle toward your lower abdomen
Drive elbows back and squeeze shoulder blades together at the end
Extend arms with control to return to start
Perform 10–12 reps

name: Rows Hoch
description: High horizontal row targeting the upper back and rear deltoids.
instructions: Stand or sit at a cable station set at upper chest height
Pull the handle toward your upper chest or chin
Keep elbows flared out to shoulder height
Squeeze upper back at the end of the pull
Perform 10–12 reps

name: Reverse Butterflies
description: Rear delt isolation targeting the back of the shoulders and upper back.
instructions: Sit at a pec deck machine facing inward, or hinge forward holding dumbbells
Start with arms forward, slight bend at elbows
Pull arms back in a wide arc out to the sides
Squeeze rear delts and upper back at the end
Return slowly to start — 12–15 reps

name: Back Extension
description: Lower back extension exercise that strengthens the erector spinae and glutes.
instructions: Position yourself face-down on a back extension bench
Start with torso hanging down at about 90°
Raise torso by extending the lower back until body is straight
Squeeze glutes at the top, do not hyperextend
Lower with control — 12–15 reps

name: Flies
description: Chest isolation exercise using a wide arc to stretch and squeeze the pecs.
instructions: Lie on a bench holding dumbbells above chest, palms facing each other
Lower arms out to the sides in a wide arc with a slight elbow bend
Feel the stretch across the chest at the bottom
Bring arms back together in the same arc, squeezing chest
Perform 12–15 reps

name: Incline Flies Low Chest
description: Incline dumbbell fly targeting the lower portion of the chest.
instructions: Set bench to a slight incline (15–30°)
Hold dumbbells above chest with a slight elbow bend
Lower arms out to the sides in a wide arc
Focus on feeling the stretch in the lower chest
Bring arms back together overhead — 12–15 reps

name: Decline Chest Press
description: Pressing movement on a decline bench targeting the lower chest.
instructions: Lie on a decline bench with feet secured
Hold dumbbells or barbell at chest level, elbows at 45°
Press the weight upward until arms are extended
Lower with control to chest level
Perform 8–12 reps

name: Dumbbell Chest Press
description: Classic pressing movement targeting the chest, shoulders, and triceps.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench holding a dumbbell in each hand at chest level
Press both dumbbells upward until arms are fully extended
Keep a slight bend in elbows at the top
Lower with control, feeling the chest stretch at the bottom
Perform 8–12 reps

name: Bankdrücken
description: Barbell bench press — the fundamental compound chest exercise.
instructions: Lie on a flat bench with eyes below the bar
Grip bar slightly wider than shoulder-width
Unrack and lower bar to mid-chest with control
Press bar back up to starting position
Perform 5–8 reps — use a spotter with heavy weight

name: Gewichtsscheibe Press
description: Chest press with a weight plate to build grip strength and chest power.
instructions: Lie on a bench holding a weight plate at chest with both hands on the edges
Press the plate directly upward until arms are extended
Keep hands flat against the plate throughout
Lower with control back to chest level
Perform 12–15 reps

name: Overhead Chest Curls
description: Overhead dumbbell movement that targets the upper chest.
instructions: Lie on a bench with dumbbells extended overhead
Lower the weights in a curling arc toward the top of your head
Keep arms parallel, feel the upper chest engage
Curl weights back to overhead position
Perform 12–15 reps

name: Lateral Raises
description: Shoulder isolation targeting the medial deltoid for shoulder width.
instructions: Stand holding dumbbells at sides, slight bend in elbows
Raise both arms to the sides until they reach shoulder height
Keep wrists neutral and avoid shrugging
Lower with control back to sides
Perform 12–15 reps

name: Shoulder Press
description: Overhead pressing movement for overall shoulder and upper body strength.
instructions: Hold dumbbells or barbell at shoulder height, palms facing forward
Brace core and press weights straight overhead
Fully extend arms at the top
Lower with control back to shoulder level
Perform 8–12 reps

name: Shoulder Front Raises
description: Anterior deltoid isolation using a forward raise motion.
instructions: Stand holding dumbbells at sides, palms facing back
Raise both arms straight forward to shoulder height
Keep a slight bend in the elbows and avoid swinging
Lower with control
Perform 12–15 reps

name: Shoulder Pulls
description: Horizontal pulling movement targeting rear delts and upper back.
instructions: Hold a resistance band or cable at shoulder height
Pull toward your face, spreading hands apart
Drive elbows back and squeeze rear delts
Return slowly to starting position
Perform 12–15 reps

name: Reverse Fly
description: Bent-over fly that isolates the rear deltoid and upper back.
instructions: Stand with a slight forward lean, dumbbells hanging below chest
Raise both arms out to the sides in a wide arc
Keep a slight bend in the elbows and lead with the elbows
Squeeze rear delts at the top
Lower with control — 12–15 reps

name: Shoulder Shrugs
description: Trap isolation exercise to build upper trapezius size and strength.
instructions: Stand holding dumbbells or barbell at sides
Shrug both shoulders straight up toward your ears
Hold briefly at the top, fully contracting the traps
Lower with control, fully stretching the traps
Perform 12–15 reps

name: Upright Rows
description: Vertical pull targeting the upper traps and side deltoids.
instructions: Stand holding dumbbells or barbell in front of thighs
Pull weight straight up along your body toward the chin
Lead with your elbows, keeping them above the wrists
Do not pull higher than the chin
Lower with control — 10–12 reps

name: Crunches
description: Classic ab exercise that targets the rectus abdominis.
instructions: Lie on your back with knees bent, feet flat, hands behind head
Brace core and curl head and shoulders off the floor
Exhale at the top, do not pull on your neck
Lower with control back to start
Perform 15–20 reps

name: Planks
description: Full-body isometric hold that builds core stability and endurance.
instructions: Start on forearms and toes, elbows below shoulders
Keep body in a straight line from head to heels
Brace core, squeeze glutes, breathe steadily
Avoid letting hips sag or rise
Hold for 30–60 seconds

name: Leg Raises
description: Lower ab exercise performed by raising straight legs from lying position.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with legs extended
Place hands under your lower back for support
Raise both legs to 90° keeping lower back pressed down
Lower legs slowly back toward the floor without touching
Perform 12–15 reps

name: Flutter Kicks
description: Alternating leg movement targeting the lower abs and hip flexors.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with legs extended, hands under glutes
Raise both legs a few centimetres off the floor
Rapidly alternate kicking each leg up and down in small motions
Keep lower back pressed into the floor
Perform for 20–30 seconds

name: Side Crunches
description: Oblique-targeting crunch variation performed from a side-lying position.
instructions: Lie on your side with legs stacked
Place top hand behind your head
Crunch sideways, lifting head and shoulder off the floor
Squeeze obliques at the top
Lower with control — 15 reps per side

name: Curls
description: Classic bicep curl for building arm size and elbow flexor strength.
instructions: Stand holding dumbbells at sides with palms facing forward
Curl both weights toward your shoulders by bending at the elbow
Squeeze biceps at the top
Lower with control back to starting position
Perform 10–12 reps

name: Wrist Curls
description: Wrist flexor exercise to strengthen forearms and improve grip.
instructions: Sit with forearms resting on thighs, palms facing up, holding weights
Lower the weight by extending wrists toward the floor
Curl wrists upward as far as possible
Keep forearms flat on thighs throughout
Perform 15–20 reps

name: Wrist Extension
description: Wrist extensor exercise for forearm balance and injury prevention.
instructions: Sit with forearms on thighs, palms facing down, holding weights
Lower weights by bending wrists toward the floor
Extend wrists upward as far as possible
Keep forearms flat on thighs throughout
Perform 15–20 reps

name: Hammer Curls
description: Neutral-grip curl targeting the brachialis and brachioradialis alongside biceps.
instructions: Stand holding dumbbells at sides, palms facing inward
Curl both weights toward shoulders with a neutral grip
Keep elbows close to sides and avoid swinging
Lower with control
Perform 10–12 reps

name: Spider Curls
description: Incline bench bicep curl that eliminates momentum for strict isolation.
instructions: Lie chest-down on a 45° incline bench
Let arms hang straight down, holding dumbbells
Curl the weights toward your face, bending only at the elbows
Squeeze biceps at the top
Lower with full extension — 10–12 reps

name: Preacher Curls
description: Bicep curl on a preacher bench for strict form and peak contraction.
instructions: Sit at a preacher bench with upper arms resting on the pad
Hold a barbell or dumbbell with underhand grip
Curl the weight toward your chin without lifting arms off the pad
Squeeze at the top, then lower fully
Perform 10–12 reps

name: Tricep Push Downs
description: Cable exercise for tricep isolation by pushing the attachment downward.
instructions: Stand at a cable machine with rope or bar attachment at head height
Grip with both hands, elbows tucked at sides
Push the attachment down until arms are fully extended
Squeeze triceps at the bottom, then return slowly
Perform 12–15 reps

name: Single Arm Push Downs
description: Unilateral cable push-down to isolate each tricep individually.
instructions: Stand at a cable machine with a single handle attachment
Grip with one hand, elbow tucked at your side
Push down until arm is fully extended
Squeeze tricep at the bottom and return slowly
Perform 12–15 reps per side

name: Single Arm Triceps Extension
description: Overhead single-arm extension for full tricep stretch and contraction.
instructions: Hold a dumbbell in one hand overhead with arm extended
Bend elbow to lower the dumbbell behind your head
Keep upper arm stationary and close to your head
Extend arm back overhead to full extension
Perform 10–12 reps per side

name: Samurai Extensions
description: Two-handed overhead tricep extension for full tricep stretch and contraction.
instructions: Hold a single dumbbell with both hands overhead
Lower the dumbbell behind your head by bending both elbows
Keep elbows close together and pointing forward
Extend arms back overhead to full lockout
Perform 12–15 reps

name: African Curls
description: Bicep curl variation with a rotating wrist motion for full muscle engagement.
instructions: Stand holding dumbbells at sides, palms facing back
Curl the weight up while rotating wrists outward (supinating)
End with palms facing up at the top of the curl
Lower with control, rotating back to starting position
Perform 10–12 reps

name: Butterfly
description: Hip-opening stretch targeting the inner thighs and hip flexors.
instructions: Sit on the floor and bring the soles of your feet together
Let knees drop out to the sides
Hold your feet and sit tall with a straight back
Gently press knees toward the floor
Hold for 30–60 seconds

name: Butterfly Holds
description: Isometric hold at the butterfly position to deepen the hip-opening stretch.
instructions: Sit in butterfly position with soles of feet together
Place hands on knees and gently press them toward the floor
Hold at the point of mild resistance
Breathe steadily and relax into the position
Hold for 30–60 seconds

name: Couch Stretch
description: Hip flexor and quad stretch performed against a wall or couch.
instructions: Kneel with one shin against a wall or couch behind you
Step the other foot forward into a lunge position
Drive hips forward and upright until you feel the hip flexor stretch
Keep torso tall and core lightly engaged
Hold for 60–90 seconds per side

name: Wrist Circles
description: Mobility drill for the wrists to improve range of motion and joint health.
instructions: Extend arms in front at shoulder height
Make a loose fist with both hands
Rotate wrists in large circles — 10 rotations clockwise
Reverse direction for 10 rotations counter-clockwise
Repeat 2–3 times

name: Cobra
description: Spinal extension stretch that opens the chest and stretches the abs.
instructions: Lie face down with hands under shoulders, elbows bent
Press palms into the floor and lift your chest up
Keep hips on the floor and elbows slightly bent
Look straight ahead and relax the lower back
Hold for 20–30 seconds

name: Cat Cow
description: Spinal mobility drill alternating between flexion and extension.
instructions: Start on hands and knees, wrists below shoulders, knees below hips
Inhale and drop belly toward floor, lifting head and tailbone (Cow)
Exhale and round spine toward the ceiling, tucking chin and pelvis (Cat)
Move fluidly between the two positions
Repeat for 10–15 slow reps

name: Child Pose
description: Resting stretch that opens the hips and releases lower back tension.
instructions: Kneel on the floor and sit back toward your heels
Reach arms forward and lower your forehead to the floor
Let the chest sink toward the floor
Breathe deeply into the back
Hold for 30–60 seconds

name: 90-90 Stretch
description: Hip mobility drill targeting internal and external rotation in both hips.
instructions: Sit with both knees bent at 90° — one leg in front, one to the side
Keep torso upright and both sit bones on the floor
Lean gently forward over the front shin to feel the stretch
Hold for 30–45 seconds, then switch sides
Repeat 2–3 times per side

name: Seated Good Mornings
description: Seated forward fold targeting hamstring flexibility and lower back mobility.
instructions: Sit on the floor with legs extended straight in front
Sit tall and brace core lightly
Hinge forward at the hips, reaching hands toward your feet
Keep back as flat as possible
Hold for 30–60 seconds

name: Pogo Jumps
description: Rapid small hops on the spot to train ankle stiffness and reactive strength.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart, slight bend in knees
Jump using only ankle and calf spring — minimal knee bend
Land and immediately jump again with minimal ground contact time
Keep torso upright and arms relaxed
Perform 20–30 reps

name: Bounce Jumps
description: Moderate-height jumps focusing on elastic energy and landing mechanics.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart
Load the hips slightly and jump upward
Land softly with bent knees to absorb impact
Immediately use the elastic rebound to jump again
Perform 10–15 reps

name: Box Jumps
description: Explosive jump onto a box to develop lower body power and landing control.
instructions: Stand facing a sturdy box (30–60 cm)
Swing arms back and bend knees to load the jump
Explode upward and forward to land on the box with both feet
Land softly with bent knees in an athletic position
Step back down and repeat — 5–8 reps

name: Seated Jumps
description: Jump from a seated position to eliminate the stretch reflex and test raw power.
instructions: Sit on a box with hips at 90°, feet flat on floor
Without rocking forward, drive through feet and jump upward
Extend hips, knees, and ankles fully at takeoff
Land softly with bent knees
Reset and repeat — 5–8 reps

name: Explosive Squats
description: Squat variation with a powerful upward drive to build lower body power.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart
Lower into a squat until thighs are parallel
Explode upward with maximum force through legs and hips
Land softly or stop at the top with full intent
Perform 6–10 reps with full effort

name: Split Jumps
description: Plyometric lunge jump that switches legs in the air for power and coordination.
instructions: Start in a lunge position with one foot forward
Jump upward explosively, switching leg positions in the air
Land in the opposite lunge position, absorbing impact softly
Immediately jump again to switch back
Perform 8–12 reps per side

name: Explosive Leg Switching
description: Rapid alternating leg switches that develop speed and lower body power.
instructions: Stand on one leg with the other raised, hip at 90°
Explosively switch legs, driving the raised knee down and the other knee up
Minimize ground contact time between switches
Stay light on your feet and keep core tight
Perform for 20–30 seconds

name: Depth Drops
description: Step off a box and land softly to train impact absorption and stiffness.
instructions: Stand on a box (30–50 cm), toes at the edge
Step off (do not jump) and drop to the floor
Land with both feet simultaneously, absorbing with bent knees
Focus on a quiet, controlled landing
Perform 5–8 reps

name: Broad Jumps
description: Horizontal jump for maximum distance to train triple extension and power.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart
Load hips back and swing arms behind
Explode forward and upward, swinging arms forward for momentum
Land with both feet, soft knees in a balanced position
Perform 5–8 reps

name: Tuck Jumps
description: Explosive jump pulling knees to chest at peak height to develop power and coordination.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart
Jump as high as possible, pulling both knees toward the chest at the top
Release legs to land softly with bent knees
Absorb the landing and immediately repeat
Perform 8–10 reps

name: Burpees
description: Full-body conditioning movement combining a squat, plank, push-up, and jump.
instructions: Stand, then squat down and place hands on the floor
Jump or step feet back into a push-up position
Perform a push-up (optional)
Jump or step feet back to your hands and stand up
Jump with arms overhead to complete one rep — 10–15 reps

name: Side Jumps
description: Lateral jumps over a line to build lateral power and landing stability.
instructions: Stand on one side of a line or low obstacle
Jump laterally to the other side with both feet
Land softly with bent knees and immediately jump back
Keep a short, reactive ground contact time
Perform 10 reps per side

name: Skater Jumps
description: Single-leg lateral jumps mimicking a speed skater to build lateral power.
instructions: Stand on one leg
Explode laterally to the other side, landing on the opposite single leg
Allow the trail leg to swing behind for balance
Absorb the landing and immediately push off to the other side
Perform 10–12 reps per side

name: Single Leg Jumps
description: Unilateral jump to develop single-leg power and landing stability.
instructions: Stand on one leg with a slight knee bend
Load the hip and jump upward as high as possible
Land on the same leg with a soft knee bend
Stabilize before the next rep
Perform 5–8 reps per side

name: Lunge Jumps Alternating
description: Alternating plyometric lunges with an explosive jump between each rep.
instructions: Start in a lunge position with one foot forward
Jump upward and switch legs in the air
Land in the opposite lunge position, absorbing softly
Immediately explode up to switch again
Perform 10–12 reps per side

name: Bounding
description: Exaggerated running strides covering maximum distance per step to build elastic power.
instructions: Begin with a short jog to build momentum
Push off one leg with maximum force, reaching forward with the opposite leg
Aim for as much distance as possible per stride
Land on the opposite foot and continue in a bounding pattern
Perform for 20–30 meters per set

name: Heel Walks
description: Walking on heels to strengthen the tibialis anterior and improve dorsiflexion.
instructions: Stand tall and lift toes off the ground
Walk forward on your heels for 20–30 meters
Take small, controlled steps keeping balance
Keep core engaged and torso upright
Rest and repeat 2–3 times

name: Toe Walks
description: Walking on tiptoes to strengthen the calves and improve ankle plantar flexion.
instructions: Rise onto the balls of your feet
Walk forward on your tiptoes for 20–30 meters
Take small, controlled steps
Keep core engaged and torso upright
Rest and repeat 2–3 times

name: Ankle Hops
description: Small rapid hops using only ankles to strengthen the calves and Achilles tendon.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart
Hop in place using only your ankles — keep knees mostly straight
Land on the balls of your feet and immediately hop again
Keep ground contact time as short as possible
Perform 20–30 rapid hops

name: Explosive Pushups
description: Push-up with explosive power, pushing up fast enough to briefly leave the floor.
instructions: Start in standard push-up position
Lower chest to the floor under control
Explode upward with maximum force, pushing hands off the floor
Land softly and immediately lower into the next rep
Perform 5–10 reps

name: Clap Pushups
description: Push-up with a mid-air clap to develop upper body power and coordination.
instructions: Start in standard push-up position
Lower chest to the floor with control
Explode upward with enough power to clap hands mid-air
Catch yourself with soft elbows and lower for the next rep
Perform 5–8 reps

name: Med Ball Chest Pass
description: Standing chest throw against a wall to develop upper body explosive power.
instructions: Stand 1–2 meters from a wall holding med ball at chest
Step forward and explosively push the ball into the wall
Fully extend both arms at the point of release
Catch the rebound and immediately reset
Perform 8–12 reps

name: Med Ball Slam
description: Full-body explosive throw of a med ball into the ground for power and conditioning.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, med ball overhead
Rise onto toes and brace core
Slam the ball into the ground with maximum force
Catch on the bounce or pick it up
Repeat for 8–12 reps

name: Russian Twists
description: Seated rotational core exercise targeting the obliques and deep core muscles.
instructions: Sit with knees bent and lean back to about 45°
Hold a weight at chest level with both hands
Rotate torso to one side, touching the weight toward the floor
Rotate to the other side to complete one rep
Perform 20 total reps (10 per side)

name: Trunk Rotation
description: Standing core rotation drill for spinal mobility and rotational strength.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, arms crossed or holding a stick
Rotate upper body to one side while keeping hips facing forward
Return to center and rotate to the other side
Control the movement, do not bounce at end range
Perform 15–20 reps per side

name: Alternating Plate Turns
description: Standing plate rotation that develops core anti-rotation and rotational power.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a weight plate at chest
Rotate to one side, extending arms straight out
Return to center and rotate to the other side
Keep hips facing forward and rotate only the torso
Perform 12–15 reps per side

name: Cable Woodchop
description: Diagonal cable pull from high to low that builds rotational core power.
instructions: Set cable at high position and stand sideways to the machine
Grip the handle with both hands above the shoulder
Pull the cable diagonally across the body to the opposite hip
Keep arms relatively straight and rotate through the core
Perform 10–12 reps per side

name: Med Ball Throws Alternating
description: Alternating rotational throws against a wall to build explosive core power.
instructions: Stand sideways to a wall, holding a med ball at hip level
Rotate away from the wall to load up
Rotate back explosively and throw the ball into the wall
Catch the rebound and immediately set up the next throw
Perform 10 reps per side

name: Shoulder Back Rotation
description: Rotational shoulder mobility drill to improve thoracic rotation and posture.
instructions: Stand or sit upright with arms extended to the sides at shoulder height
Rotate one arm forward and the other backward simultaneously
Keep core stable and movement controlled
Alternate directions fluidly
Perform 10–15 reps per side
